Tests if multiplying `undefined` by a `Number` object results in `NaN`.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that uses the `assert` function to verify a specific behavior in JavaScript. It checks whether the result of multiplying `undefined` by a `Number` object containing the value `1` is `NaN` (Not-a-Number). The use of `isNaN` ensures that the expression evaluates to `true` if the result is indeed `NaN`. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on a single test case to validate the behavior of JavaScript's type coercion and arithmetic operations.
